content: To account for the Drexler-Nussbaum entropic leakage (DNL) factor when calibrating your calorimeter, apply the correction from the DNL table by adjusting your measured heat values downward by the specified percentage (around 4%) to compensate for entropy-related heat losses through imperfect insulation; if the table is missing, use the standard DNL correction formula or consult the original Drexler-Nussbaum publication for the exact entropic leakage values based on your calorimeter’s design and conditions.
